back_translation: As fellow workers of God, we therefore plead with you that you do not receive God's grace in a fruitless manner.
For he says, 'In the time of favor, I listened to you, and in the day of salvation, I helped you.' Behold, now is the time of favor; behold, now is the day of salvation.
We are not setting up any obstacle in any matter to anyone, so that our ministry would not be criticized.
On the contrary, as servants of God, we commend ourselves in every matter: in great endurance, in afflictions, in distresses, in pressings;
in beatings, in imprisonments, in riots, in laborious work, in lack of sleep, in lack of food;
in purity of heart, in knowledge, in long-restraint, in mercy, in the Holy Spirit, in love that is genuine;
in the word of truth, in the power of God; with weapons of righteousness which are in the right hand and the left hand;
through glory and dishonor, through slander and praise; viewed as deceivers, but we are genuine;
viewed as those whom no one knows, but actually well-known; as people about to die, but behold, we are still living; disciplined with punishment, but not put to death;
as people who are sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as people who are poor, yet making many people rich; as people who have nothing, yet possessing everything.
Corinthians, our mouth has been opened to speak with you. Our heart is also opened wide.
You are not restricted in our heart, but you are restricted in your own love.
As an equal recompense — I speak as if speaking to children — open your hearts widely as well.
Do not be unequally yoked together with unbelievers in an unsuitable way. For how can righteousness and lawlessness participate together? Or how can light have fellowship with darkness?
How can Christ have agreement with Beliar? Or how can a believer have a share with one who does not believe?

How can the temple of God have an agreement with idols? For we are the temple of the living God, just as God said, 'I will dwell among them and walk among them, I will be their God, and they will be my people.'
'Therefore, come out from among them, and be separated, says the Lord, and do not touch any unclean thing, and I will receive you.'
'And I will be a Father to you, and you will be sons and daughters to me, says the Lord, the Almighty.'
